result:
Subject: Yesterday's Payments Outage — What Happened and What We're Doing

Hi there,

Yesterday, our payments service was degraded for approximately four hours, and some of you saw transactions fail outright. That's not the experience you trust us to deliver, and we're sorry. If a payment of yours didn't go through, we understand the frustration and the downstream impact it may have caused — we own this.

The root cause was a misconfigured cache invalidation rule that behaved correctly under normal load but collapsed when traffic spiked, starving the payment path of valid session data. Once identified, we corrected the rule, flushed the affected cache tiers, and verified end-to-end transaction health before declaring the incident resolved.

To prevent this from happening again, we're making two concrete changes: all cache and routing configuration changes will now ship through canary rollouts with automated rollback thresholds, and we're adding chaos testing in staging that specifically simulates traffic-spike conditions against cache subsystems. These are not future aspirations — work is already underway, and we'll share progress transparently.

As an acknowledgment of the disruption, we're applying a goodwill credit to the accounts of impacted users; you'll see it reflected within the next few business days with no action required on your part. If you had a failed transaction that needs direct attention, please reply to this email and we'll make it right.

Sincerely,
The Payments Team
